title: Weighted Loss Functions
concept: Weighted loss assigns a higher mathematical penalty to errors made on the minority class during training. This forces the optimizer to treat minority errors seriously, without needing to copy or delete any image files.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main disadvantage of undersampling a massive majority class?

options.text: Information loss: you discard valuable training data that could help the model learn
It causes severe overfitting on minority duplicates
It increases the spatial size of the feature maps

cards.title: Severe Penalties
cards.body: Instead of duplicating or deleting images to balance the dataset, what if we changed the rules of the game? What if getting a rare object wrong cost the model far more points than getting a common object wrong? This is the core idea of class weights.

cards.title: Class Weighted Loss
cards.body: Weighted loss multiplies the loss calculation by a class weight factor. If a dataset has 90% Class A and 10% Class B, we can assign a weight of **1.0** to Class A and **9.0** to Class B. A mistake on Class B now triggers 9 times the gradient update penalty.

cards.title: The high-stakes penalty kick
cards.body: Imagine playing soccer. Scoring a regular goal is worth 1 point (majority class). Scoring a goal from the halfway line is worth 10 points (minority class). Because the halfway shot is worth so much, the player will practice and focus on it intensely, despite its rarity.

cards.title: Cancer Detection Weights
cards.body: In cancer screening, missing a tumor (False Negative) is far worse than flagging a healthy scan for a follow-up (False Positive). We set the loss weight for tumors high so the model is heavily penalized for missing them, making it highly sensitive.

cards.title: It doesn't create new features
cards.body: Weighted loss is incredibly elegant because it changes zero files on disk. However, it cannot invent new features. If your minority class only has 5 images, weighting them by 1000x will just force the model to memorize those 5 images, leading to extreme overfitting.

cards.title: Standard vs. Weighted Loss
cards.body: How error penalties are calculated in backpropagation:

left.text:
Every class error costs 1.0
Model focuses on majority to win
Neglects rare classes

right.text:
Minority error costs e.g. 10.0
Model highly penalized for rare mistakes
Forces balanced learning

exercises.prompt: How does weighted loss help handle class imbalance?

options.text: It assigns a higher penalty to errors made on the minority class, forcing larger gradient updates
It deletes images from the majority class automatically
It increases the number of filters in the first convolutional layer
It resizes all images to be twice as large during training

exercises.explain: Correct! Weighted loss increases the penalty on minority errors, driving larger updates for those classes.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Weighted loss requires duplicating minority images on your hard drive before training begins.
exercises.explain: False. Weighted loss is purely mathematical, altering the loss function calculation in memory without changing any files.

exercises.sentence: Weighted loss adjusts gradient update penalties during backpropagation, acting on the ___ level instead of the data level.
exercises.explain: Weighted loss changes the loss function calculation, balancing training mathematically.

exercises.prompt: In what scenario is assigning a massive weight (e.g. 1000x) to a minority class dangerous?

options.text: If the minority class has very few samples, the model will severely overfit to those specific images
It will cause the model to automatically delete the classification head
It will convert all weights to zero instantly

exercises.explain: Correct! Extreme weights on tiny classes lead to intense overfitting on those specific samples.

exercises.prompt: You are training a self-driving car model to detect pedestrians (rare) and empty roads (common). If you assign a weight of 100x to pedestrian detection loss, what is the expected side effect?

options.text: The model will become highly sensitive to pedestrians, but may increase False Positives (flagging shadows or mailboxes as pedestrians)
The model will completely stop detecting roads
The training loop will run 100 times slower
Pedestrian bounding boxes will automatically become 100 times larger

exercises.explain: Exactly! High sensitivity often increases False Positives. Next: using data augmentation strategically to balance classes!
